doubled Vighatis to the Sun's longitude and also add 120
degrees. Expunge multiples of360. The result gives the posi
tion of Pranapada.
For example, the birth Vighatis are 658. Multiplying this
by two, we get 1316. The Sun is in a cardinal sign at 278 de-

Traditional Methods 17
gree.IO'. Adding the two we get 1594 deg.10'. The product
is 154 deg.10'. Pranapada is Kanya on 4 deg.10'. This is no
where near the lagna at 658 Vighatis. The lagna is Mina 25
deg.46'.
The lagna at birth can be a trine to Pranapada or the sev
enth from it. This is no consolation since the exact time of
birth cannot be known from Pranapada.
The Uttara Kalamrita theory and its modification as
propounded by Sri Rajagopala Ayyer can be of some help
to us.
Vl. Another theory argues that the navamsa signs occu
pied by the lord oflagna and by the Moon can be the lagna at
birth. This is a vague theory. lt can at best help us when the
calculated lagna is at the beginning or at the end of a sign.
VII.
